overview.description: An AI-powered HR Management System that uses a LangGraph-based agentic architecture with RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) to answer HR policy questions, manage employee data, handle leave applications, track onboarding, send emails, and provide analytics. The system has TWO frontends: a Streamlit app (primary) and a React+Vite app (secondary). The backend uses FastAPI to serve the React frontend. Both frontends share the same agent/tools/RAG pipeline.

backend.framework: FastAPI (for React frontend API), Streamlit (standalone UI)

variable.name: AI_KEY
variable.description: OpenAI API key. Also checked as OPENAI_API_KEY.

virtual_env.note: Python virtual environment, not committed to git

file.name: .env
file.description: Environment variables (API keys, email config)

file.name: .gitignore
file.description: Git ignore rules

file.name: README.md
file.description: Project documentation (9KB)

file.name: README_old.md
file.description: Previous version of README

file.name: requirements.txt
file.description: Python dependencies

file.name: package.json
file.description: Root-level npm (only dompurify)

file.name: config.py
file.description: Central configuration — loads .env, defines all paths and API keys

file.name: app.py
file.description: STREAMLIT FRONTEND — main entry point, 230 lines

file.name: ui_redesign.py
file.description: UI components — CSS + HTML templates for Streamlit, 717 lines

file.name: ingest_docs.py
file.description: Document ingestion pipeline — PDF/MD/TXT/DOCX/CSV/XLSX to ChromaDB, 378 lines

file.name: seed_db.py
file.description: Database seeder — creates SQLite schema + 20 mock employees, 129 lines

file.name: extract_nda.py
file.description: Utility script to test PDF text extraction

file.name: hr_ui_preview.html
file.description: Static HTML preview of the UI design

file.name: __init__.py
file.description: Empty init

file.name: graph.py
file.description: LangGraph workflow — agent node, tools node, routing logic, 219 lines

file.name: state.py
file.description: AgentState TypedDict — messages + sources, 7 lines

file.name: tools_registry.py
file.description: Tool registry — imports and returns all 17 tools, 48 lines

dir.name: backend
dir.description: FastAPI REST API for React frontend

file.name: api.py
file.description: FastAPI app — /api/chat, /api/documents, /api/upload, /api/ingest, /api/document-preview, 210 lines

file.name: index.html
file.description: HTML entry — loads Google Fonts (Inter, DM Mono)

file.name: package.json
file.description: Node dependencies

file.name: vite.config.js
file.description: Vite config — port 3000, proxy /api to localhost:8000

file.name: main.jsx
file.description: React entry — renders App into #root

file.name: App.jsx
file.description: Main React component — sidebar, chat, preview panel, 492 lines

file.name: index.css
file.description: Complete CSS design system — dark theme, 816 lines

file.name: __init__.py
file.description: Empty init

file.name: base.py
file.description: hr_tool decorator — wraps langchain_core.tools.tool

file.name: retrieval.py
file.description: RAG search — ChromaDB + reranker + source tracking, 393 lines

file.name: employee_tool.py
file.description: Employee lookup/search/team tools, 91 lines

file.name: email_tool.py
file.description: SMTP email tools, 68 lines

file.name: leave_tool.py
file.description: Leave management tools (apply/approve/reject/balance), 187 lines

file.name: onboarding_tool.py
file.description: Onboarding checklist tools, 114 lines

file.name: analytics_tool.py
file.description: Analytics/reporting tools, 50 lines

file.name: document_viewer.py
file.description: Document preview renderer — PDF/TXT/MD/DOCX/Excel with highlighting, 307 lines

file.name: source_preview.py
file.description: Alternative source preview utilities (citations, line numbers), 165 lines

file.name: __init__.py
file.description: Empty — RAG logic lives in tools/retrieval.py

file.name: hr_database.sqlite
file.description: SQLite DB with employees, leaves, onboarding tables

dir.name: documents
dir.description: HR policy documents for RAG ingestion

file.description: Loads .env and defines all shared paths/keys
file.note: Every other module imports from config.py. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for paths and keys.

agent_architecture.description: The agent uses LangGraph's StateGraph with two nodes: "agent" and "tools". The agent node calls the LLM (with all tools bound). If the LLM returns tool_calls, the router sends execution to the "tools" node. After tools execute, control returns to "agent" for a final response. If no tools are called, the graph ends.

field.name: messages
field.description: Chat message history, accumulated via operator.add

field.name: sources
field.description: Source documents from RAG retrieval, accumulated

initialization.step: tools = get_all_tools() — loads all 17 tools from tools_registry

node.name: agent
node.description: Prepends a SystemMessage with SYSTEM_PROMPT if not present. Invokes llm_with_tools with the full message history. Returns the LLM response (which may contain tool_calls).

node.name: tools
node.description: Iterates over tool_calls from the last message. Looks up each tool by name from the tools list. Invokes each tool with its arguments. If result is a dict with "sources", extends state sources. Returns ToolMessage for each call.

edge.description: After tool execution, return to agent for final answer

graph_definition.system_prompt: The SYSTEM_PROMPT defines the agent as an HR Assistant with specific tool selection rules. It includes dynamically generated tool descriptions with parameter info. Tool selection is guided by 8 categories: Policy, Employee, Leave Balance, Apply Leave, Approve/Reject Leave, Onboarding, Emails, Analytics. The agent declines non-HR queries (math, weather, sports, etc.). Answer format includes source citations: [Source: filename | Section: X]

function.name: get_all_tools
function.description: Returns all 17 HR tools for the agent. Imported from tools/ package.

tool_module.description: Defines the hr_tool decorator which is a simple wrapper around langchain_core.tools.tool

The most complex tool. Implements a multi-stage RAG pipeline: 1. Policy type detection from query keywords 2. ChromaDB vector search (embeddings) 3. Policy-type filtering/boosting 4. Cross-encoder reranking 5. Jaccard verification scoring 6. Source grouping (PDF by file, text by line ranges) 7. Deduplication

method.name: extract_policy_type
method.description: Detects policy type from query using keyword matching
method.policy_types: whistle blower, confidentiality, it asset, leave, moonlighting, remote work, onboarding, code of conduct

method.name: rerank_chunks
method.params: query: str, chunks: List[Dict], top_k: int=5
method.description: Reranks using CrossEncoder. Adds relevance_score to each chunk.

method.name: filter_by_policy_type
method.description: Boosts chunks matching detected policy type to top of results

method.name: verify_source_match
method.description: Jaccard similarity between query words and content words (4+ char words)

method.name: retrieve_with_sources
method.params: query: str, k: int=15, final_k: int=7
method.description: Full retrieval pipeline: embed query → ChromaDB search → policy filter → rerank → verify → sort by combined score → return top final_k

tool_function.name: search_policies
tool_function.description: Main RAG tool. Returns {"answer": str, "sources": list}.

pipeline.step: Retrieve chunks via retrieve_with_sources(query, k=15, final_k=10)
Separate PDF chunks (have page number) from text chunks
Group PDF chunks by source_file, load ALL pages of each PDF via pdfplumber
Build pdf_sources with full_content (all pages), chunks (matched text), section info
Group text chunks by source_file, merge contiguous line ranges (gap≤2, total≤50 lines)
Build answer string: "RESULT N [Source: file | Section: X | Lines: Y-Z]\n{content}"

tool_function.source_object_shape_pdf: {source_file, page, section, full_content: [{page, text}...], chunks: [str...], start_line, end_line, relevance_score, verification_score}
tool_function.source_object_shape_text: {source_file, section, segments: [{start_line, end_line, content, section}...], content, start_line, end_line}

tool_function.name: lookup_employee
tool_function.description: Search employees by name/department/designation (LIKE match). Returns name, role, email, manager, join_date, status.

tool_function.name: count_by_department
tool_function.description: Returns active employee count grouped by department.

tool_function.name: get_team
tool_function.description: Returns all direct reports of a manager.

helper.name: _send_smtp_email
helper.description: SMTP send via EMAIL_HOST:EMAIL_PORT with TLS

helper.name: _lookup_employee_email
helper.description: Looks up email from employees table by name

tool_function.name: notify_employee
tool_function.description: Looks up employee email by name, then sends email

tool_function.name: check_leave_balance
tool_function.description: Returns all leave type balances for an employee

tool_function.name: apply_leave
tool_function.description: Creates a leave record with status='Pending'. Sends email to employee and their manager. Dates must be YYYY-MM-DD. Valid types: casual, sick, earned, maternity, paternity.

tool_function.name: get_pending_leaves
tool_function.description: Returns all pending leave requests (joins leaves + employees tables)

tool_function.name: approve_leave
tool_function.description: Approves leave, deducts days from leave_balances, sends email notification

tool_function.name: reject_leave
tool_function.description: Rejects leave with reason, sends email notification

tool_function.name: get_onboarding_checklist
tool_function.description: Returns full onboarding task list with completion status for an employee

tool_function.name: mark_task_complete
tool_function.description: Marks a specific onboarding task as completed (sets status='Completed', completed_at=today)

tool_function.name: get_onboarding_progress
tool_function.description: Returns percentage of completed onboarding tasks

tool_function.name: get_leave_summary
tool_function.description: Company-wide leave usage grouped by type and status

tool_function.name: get_department_summary
tool_function.description: Department stats: headcount + average tenure in years

table.name: employees
table.seed_data: 20 employees across Engineering, Product, HR, Sales, Finance departments

table.name: leave_balances
table.seed_data: 100 rows (20 employees × 5 leave types). Default: casual=12, sick=10, earned=15, maternity=182, paternity=15

table.name: employee_onboarding
table.seed_data: Assigned to employees 3, 16, 20. Employee 3 has first 4 tasks completed.

ingestion.description: Reads all files from documents/ directory, chunks them, embeds with SentenceTransformer, and upserts into ChromaDB collection "hr_docs_v2". Run via: python ingest_docs.py Also triggered from Streamlit UI (subprocess) or FastAPI /api/ingest endpoint.

extractor.name: extract_text_from_pdf
extractor.description: Extracts text per page, sub-chunks each page. Metadata includes page number.
extractor.metadata: source, section ("Page N"), page (int), start_line (approx), end_line (approx)

extractor.name: extract_text_from_excel
extractor.description: Converts each sheet to string, sub-chunks. Section = sheet name.

extractor.name: extract_text_from_txt
extractor.description: Reads file, sub-chunks. Tracks actual line positions.

extractor.name: extract_text_from_docx
extractor.description: Extracts paragraph text, sub-chunks. Tracks line positions.

extractor.name: split_markdown_by_headers
extractor.description: Splits by markdown headers. Detects policy sections (e.g. "**8) Whistle Blower Policy**"). Tracks policy_type in metadata for better filtering during retrieval. Sub-chunks sections larger than 500 chars.
extractor.metadata: source, section (header text), policy_type, start_line, end_line

pipeline.step: For each file in documents/: extract chunks with metadata
Upsert into ChromaDB in batches of 500

streamlit_frontend.description: Primary UI. Run with: streamlit run app.py Uses a 2-column layout: chat (left) + source preview (right). Has a Streamlit sidebar with branding, quick questions, file upload, and clear button.

sidebar.component: SIDEBAR_STATUS_HTML — Green dot + "Knowledge base connected · N documents"
Upload Documents — file_uploader (pdf, md, csv, xlsx, txt, docx)
Ingest button — runs ingest_docs.py via subprocess

column.name: col_chat
column.component: Empty state HTML when no messages
Message history — user bubbles + answer cards
Answer card includes refs_html block with source citations

column.name: col_preview
column.component: Source cards for latest assistant message sources
Open/Close buttons to expand document preview

processing_flow.step: Detect greetings (hi, hello, etc.) — respond without calling agent
For real queries: graph.stream(inputs) where inputs = {messages: [HumanMessage], sources: []}
Collect response from agent events, tool call steps, and sources from tools events
Append assistant message with content, steps, sources to session_state.messages

streamlit_frontend.imports_from_ui_redesign: CUSTOM_CSS, SIDEBAR_BRAND_HTML, SIDEBAR_STATUS_HTML, SIDEBAR_SECTION_LABEL, answer_card_html, THINKING_HTML, source_doc_card_html, EMPTY_STATE_HTML

ui_styling.description: Contains all CSS and HTML template functions for the Streamlit UI. Dark theme with glassmorphism, custom fonts (DM Sans, DM Mono, Fraunces). Exports constants and functions used by app.py.

export.name: CUSTOM_CSS
export.description: Full CSS stylesheet wrapped in style tags. ~584 lines of CSS.

utils_document_viewer.description: Renders document previews in the Streamlit source panel with syntax highlighting.

function.name: resolve_doc_path
function.description: Tries multiple path candidates to find the actual file on disk

function.name: strip_citation_markers
function.description: Removes all [Source: ...] markers from LLM output via regex

function.name: deduplicate_sources
function.description: Deduplicates by filename, merging line ranges (min start, max end)

function.name: get_pdf_page_text
function.description: Extracts single page text from PDF via pdfplumber

function.name: get_full_pdf_text
function.description: Extracts all pages text from PDF

function.name: render_document_preview_html
function.description: Router: dispatches to PDF, text, data, or docx-specific renderer based on file extension

function.name: _render_pdf_preview
function.description: Renders PDF page with chunk highlighting. Uses exact-match first, then fuzzy matching (difflib.SequenceMatcher, ratio>0.7). Merges overlapping spans. Renders via components.html.

function.name: _render_text_preview
function.description: Line-by-line viewer with yellow highlighting. Highlights ALL line ranges from segments list. Shows a window from global_start-5 to global_end+10.

function.name: _render_data_preview
function.description: Shows chunk content for Excel/CSV sources via st.code

function.name: _render_docx_preview
function.description: Shows content in monospace container via components.html

utils_source_preview.description: Alternative source preview utilities. Provides citation extraction, line-number rendering, and side-by-side layout. May be partially superseded by document_viewer.py.

function.name: extract_citations
function.description: Regex parses [Source: file | Section: X | Lines Y-Z] markers

backend_api.description: FastAPI REST API that serves the React frontend. Run with: uvicorn backend.api:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 Or: python backend/api.py
backend_api.graph_loading: Lazy-loaded singleton via get_graph(). Reset on ingest.

model.name: SourceInfo
model.fields: source_file:str, section:str, start_line:int, end_line:int, content:Optional[str], score:Optional[float], full_content:Optional[list[dict]], chunks:Optional[list[str]], page:Optional[int]

endpoint.description: Sends message to LangGraph agent. Streams events, collects response + sources + steps. Deduplicates sources and strips citation markers before returning.
Lists all files in documents/ directory
Saves uploaded files to documents/ directory
Runs ingest_docs.py via subprocess. Resets cached graph.
Returns document content for preview. For .md/.txt/.docx: returns line-by-line with highlight flags. For binary: returns type info.
endpoint.response_text: {type:"text", lines:[{num, text, highlighted}], total_lines, highlight_start, highlight_end}

react_frontend.description: Alternative frontend built with React 19 + Vite. Communicates with backend/api.py. Run: cd frontend && npm run dev (starts on port 3000, proxies /api to port 8000) Must also run: uvicorn backend.api:app --port 8000

entry.description: HTML shell. Loads Inter + DM Mono fonts from Google. Mounts React into #root.

main.description: Creates React root, renders App in StrictMode, imports index.css

app.description: Single-file React application with 3-panel layout: sidebar, chat, preview.

function.name: generateHighlightedHtml
function.description: Complex highlighting engine. Collects search terms from: user query, retriever chunks, bold terms from AI answer, sentences from answer. Finds matching lines using regex. Groups into paragraph blocks. Returns snippet with context lines (±5). Uses DOMPurify to sanitize before rendering via dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

var.name: previewData
var.description: Preview state: type, lines, pages, currentIndex, chunks, answerContext

effect.description: Fetch document count on mount via GET /api/documents
Auto-scroll chat to bottom on messages/loading change

var.name: latestSources
var.description: Sources from the last assistant message

var.name: lastUserQuery
var.description: Content of the last user message

method.name: handlePageChange
method.description: Navigates between PDF pages or text segments in preview

method.name: sendMessage
method.description: POST /api/chat with message. Appends user + assistant messages to state.

method.name: togglePreview
method.description: Toggles document preview for a source card. For PDFs: uses source.full_content pages, generates highlighted HTML. For text files: uses source.content, generates highlighted HTML. Uses generateHighlightedHtml for both.

method.name: handleKeyDown
method.description: Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline

css_design_system.description: Complete dark-theme design system. Custom properties for colors, fonts, radii, transitions.

command.name: Setup Database
command.description: Creates SQLite DB with schema + seed data. Run once.

command.name: Ingest Documents
command.description: Processes documents/ into ChromaDB. Run after adding new docs.

command.name: Run FastAPI Backend
command.description: Starts API server for React frontend

command.name: Run React Frontend
command.description: Starts Vite dev server on port 3000

flow.name: User Query (Streamlit)
flow.step: User types in st.chat_input → query stored in session_state
app.py checks if greeting → respond directly without agent
Otherwise: graph.stream({messages: [HumanMessage], sources: []})
LangGraph agent_node: LLM decides which tool(s) to call
If tool_calls: tools_node executes them (e.g., search_policies → ChromaDB → rerank)
tools_node returns ToolMessages + sources to agent_node
agent_node generates final response using tool results
app.py collects full_response, steps, sources from stream events
Appends {role:"assistant", content, steps, sources} to messages
st.rerun() refreshes the UI

flow.name: User Query (React)
flow.step: User types in input → sendMessage(text) called
POST /api/chat {message: text} to FastAPI backend
backend/api.py: Same graph.stream() pipeline as Streamlit
Returns ChatResponse {answer, sources, steps}
React state updated with new assistant message
Preview panel shows latest sources

flow.name: Document Upload + Ingestion
flow.step: User uploads files via sidebar (Streamlit) or POST /api/upload (React)
Files saved to documents/ directory
User clicks Ingest (Streamlit) or POST /api/ingest (React)
ingest_docs.py runs: reads all docs, chunks, embeds, upserts to ChromaDB
Graph cache cleared so new data is available

flow.name: Source Preview
flow.step: Sources attached to each assistant message
Latest sources shown in right panel
User clicks Open on a source card
Streamlit: render_document_preview_html(source) called
React: togglePreview() generates highlighted HTML via generateHighlightedHtml()
PDF sources include full_content (all pages) for pagination
Text sources include segments with line ranges for highlighting

conventions.convention: All tools use @hr_tool (wraps @tool from langchain_core). Tools must return str or Dict[str, Any].
Sources from search_policies have: source_file, section, start_line, end_line. PDFs also have: full_content, chunks, page. Text files also have: segments, content.
Most modules use try/except ImportError with sys.path manipulation to support both direct execution and package imports.
All SQLite tools create a new connection per call via _get_conn() → sqlite3.connect(DB_PATH). No connection pooling.
LLM outputs [Source: filename | Section: X] markers. These are stripped by strip_citation_markers() before display.
All custom styling injected via st.markdown(CUSTOM_CSS, unsafe_allow_html=True). No external CSS files for Streamlit.
All styling in single index.css file using CSS custom properties. No CSS modules or styled-components.
Errors in tools return error strings (not exceptions). Agent receives error as ToolMessage content.
Always "hr_docs_v2". Deleted and recreated on each ingest run.

scenario.name: Add a new tool
scenario.step: Create a new file in tools/ (e.g., tools/my_tool.py)
Import hr_tool from tools.base and config values from config.py
Define your tool function with @hr_tool decorator
Add import in agent/tools_registry.py → get_all_tools()
Add the tool to the returned list in get_all_tools()
Update SYSTEM_PROMPT in agent/graph.py to describe when to use the new tool

scenario.name: Add a new database table
scenario.step: Add CREATE TABLE in seed_db.py
Add seed data if needed
Run: python seed_db.py (WARNING: drops all existing tables)
Create tools in tools/ that query the new table

scenario.name: Add a new page/feature to React frontend
scenario.step: Currently single-page app (App.jsx). For new pages, add React Router.
Add new components in frontend/src/
Style in frontend/src/index.css using existing design tokens
If new API needed, add endpoint in backend/api.py

scenario.name: Add a new page/feature to Streamlit frontend
scenario.step: Streamlit uses single-page architecture (app.py)
Add new UI sections in app.py layout
Add CSS in ui_redesign.py CUSTOM_CSS
Add HTML template functions in ui_redesign.py

scenario.name: Add a new document type for ingestion
scenario.step: Add extractor function in ingest_docs.py (follow existing pattern)
Add file extension check in the main() loop
Add viewer in utils/document_viewer.py render_document_preview_html()
Update Streamlit file_uploader accepted types in app.py

scenario.name: Change the LLM model
scenario.step: Update AI_MODEL in .env file
If not OpenAI-compatible, modify agent/graph.py llm initialization

scenario.name: Change embedding model
scenario.step: Update EMBEDDING_MODEL in .env
Re-run: python ingest_docs.py (re-embeds all documents)
